commit.message:
Fix game hang by correcting CPU branch timing

Branch taken (+1) and page-crossing (+2) cycle penalties were lost because
branch() incremented this.cycles but execute() overwrote it with the base
cost. Use a branchPenalty field that gets added after execute() returns.
Also remove temporary debug instrumentation from PPU and NesEmulator.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

Add component diagram in draw.io format

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

Fix game hang after screensaver by delivering APU frame counter IRQs to CPU

The APU's isIrqPending() was never checked in the main loop, so games
relying on APU IRQs for timing (e.g. Double Dragon & Battletoads) would
spin indefinitely waiting for an interrupt that never fired.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

Add APU implementation for sound support

Implement NES Audio Processing Unit with pulse, triangle, and noise
channels, wired into the CPU bus and main emulation loop. Audio output
uses javax.sound.sampled at 44100 Hz.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

Fix game freeze after title screen by adding NMI edge detection, PRG RAM, and DMA NMI handling

- Trigger NMI immediately when game enables NMI via PPUCTRL while VBlank is active
- Add 8KB PRG RAM at $6000-$7FFF so game state reads/writes work correctly
- Check for NMI during DMA transfer to avoid missing VBlank interrupts

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

Add README with architecture overview, setup, and API docs

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

Implement notification system with server cluster, workers, emulators, and load testing

- Notification server: REST API, input validation, template rendering, Redis caching, RabbitMQ routing
- Notification worker: 4 dedicated queue consumers (APNS/FCM/SMS/EMAIL) with retry logic
- Third-party emulator: simulates delivery services with 95% success rate
- Device emulator: receives notifications, WebSocket broadcast, REST stats
- Load client: seeds test data, multi-threaded load test with p95/p99 reporting
- Infrastructure: PostgreSQL, Redis, RabbitMQ, Prometheus, Grafana with pre-provisioned dashboard
- Docker Compose with service health checks and replicas
- 27 unit tests covering validation, services, controllers, consumers
- Component diagram in drawio format

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

Refine card status tint: concentrated accent (light) vs atmospheric glow (dark)

Light mode: sharper left-edge accent — stronger start (11% opacity)
with quick falloff, fading by 24% width. Looks crisp on white cards.

Dark mode: wide atmospheric wash — gentle 12% opacity spreading
across 45% of card. Creates a subtle glow on dark surfaces.

Moved gradient from inline style to CSS data-status attributes
for proper :active state handling and cleaner component code.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

Integrate agent_loop with ExperimentEnvironment and GPUPoolRunner

Replace local GPU pool, diff parsing, patch application, and experiment
execution with the shared environment.py/runners.py infrastructure.
env.step() dispatches to 6 remote H100s via SSH; asyncio.to_thread()
keeps the async event loop unblocked during ~5 min experiments.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

Solve LeetCode 643: Maximum Average Subarray I

Implemented fixed-size sliding window to find maximum average of k consecutive elements.
Build initial window sum, then slide by removing left and adding right elements.
Time: O(n), Space: O(1)

Co-Authored-By: Claude Sonnet 4.5 <noreply@anthropic.com>

commit.message:
feat(examples): Sprint 6 - Weather Systems (40 traits)

Add 5 comprehensive weather examples covering particles and phenomena.

**Weather Examples (40 traits total):**

1. particle-emitters.holo (12 particle traits) - GPU-accelerated systems
   - Rain, snow, fog, dust emitters with physics simulation
   - Fire, smoke, magic, sparkle effects with emissive lighting
   - Firefly/spark systems with flickering behavior
   - Performance: 1M+ particles @ 60 FPS via WebGPU compute

2. storm-systems.holo (5 storm traits) - Dynamic severe weather
   - Lightning bolts with procedural branching and thunder
   - Thunderstorms combining rain, wind, lightning, clouds
   - Tornadoes with vortex physics and debris systems
   - Hurricanes/typhoons with eye walls and rain bands

3. atmospheric-events.holo (4 event traits) - Rare phenomena
   - Aurora borealis with electromagnetic curtains
   - Meteor showers with sonic booms and fireballs
   - Solar eclipses with corona, diamond ring, Baily's beads
   - Rainbows with double/fog bow support and optical physics

4. natural-disasters.holo (9 disaster traits) - Catastrophic events
   - Earthquakes with seismic waves and liquefaction
   - Tsunamis with wave propagation and flooding
   - Avalanches with snow flow physics
   - Wildfires with ember transport and spread modeling
   - Volcanic eruptions with lava, pyroclastic flows, ash clouds
   - Landslides, sinkholes, geysers with realistic mechanics

5. seasonal-weather.holo (10 seasonal traits) - Climate patterns
   - Blizzards with whiteout conditions and wind chill
   - Sandstorms with haboob dust walls and AQI tracking
   - Hailstorms with damage simulation
   - Monsoons with seasonal rainfall patterns
   - Heatwaves with heat index and UV exposure
   - Cold snaps, floods, droughts, whirlpools, waterspouts
   - Solar flares with electromagnetic effects

All examples include comprehensive event handlers, interactive UI controls,
performance monitoring, and safety warnings for hazardous conditions.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Sonnet 4.5 <noreply@anthropic.com>

commit.message:
fix: rollup failed paths to common parent in scan results

Instead of showing hundreds of individual subdirectories (e.g.
Nextcloud4\Music\Beatles, Nextcloud4\3dPrinter\AR15, etc.), iteratively
collapse sibling dirs that all have errors into their common parent when
>= 3 siblings share errors. Repeat until stable.

Result: Nextcloud4 with 5970 errors surfaces as one entry instead of
hundreds of deep subdirectory paths that the user had to add one-by-one.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

refactor: planner-integrated permission suppression + explicit data flow (#203)

* refactor: move permission-denied suppression into planner

Eliminates the dual-system problem where 403s entered both remote_state
(retry/escalation pipeline) and local_issues (permission tracking),
causing false conflict escalation after 10 cycles.

Changes:
- Move denied-prefix awareness into Planner.Plan() via deniedPrefixes
  parameter — paths under denied prefixes use SyncDownloadOnly mode
- Change handle403/handlePermissionCheckError to return bool, populate
  permCache on read-only detection
- Reorder drainWorkerResults: check permissions FIRST for 403s, skip
  remote_state recording for confirmed read-only (no retry/escalation)
- Make permissionCache nil-safe with deniedPrefixes() method
- Update recheckPermissions to cache ALL issues (including unresolvable)
- Delete filterDeniedWrites, loadDeniedPrefixes, isWriteSuppressed,
  isRemoteWriteAction (logic now lives in planner)
- Dedup shortcut queries: handleRemovedShortcuts accepts []Shortcut,
  reconcileShortcutScopes uses detectShortcutCollisionsFromList directly
- Delete non-list wrapper methods: detectShortcutCollisions,
  observeShortcutContent

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

* refactor: explicit data flow, shared result drain, permission fixes

- resolveRemoteItemID: free function accepting *Baseline (no store access)
- handle403/walkPermissionBoundary/recheckPermissions: accept *Baseline param
- drainWorkerResults: accept bl/shortcuts explicitly (no lazy DB load)
- processWorkerResult: shared helper for one-shot and watch mode
- executePlan: synchronous result drain after pool.Stop() (fixes RunOnce
  never processing 403s, RecordFailure, or upload issues)
- RunOnce: hoist shortcut load for reuse in permission recheck + drain
- handleRemovedShortcuts: accept []Shortcut param (shortcut query dedup)
- permCache nil-safe methods, deniedPrefixes() on cache
- handle403/handlePermissionCheckError return bool
- Tests for processWorkerResult, recheck cache population, denied moves

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

---------

commit.message:
fix: use tangent.w flag to distinguish smooth normals from import tangents

Imported meshes have tangent.w=±1 (bitangent sign), which the shader
was misreading as smooth normals. Now checks abs(w)<0.5 to detect our
baked smooth normals (w=0). Also reverts to object-space expansion
(simpler, reliable with smooth normals) and fixes smoother double-bake
when multiple controllers exist on the same renderer.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

commit.message:
fix: use shallowRef for JsonRpcProvider to prevent Proxy errors

Vue's ref() wraps objects in a reactive Proxy, but ethers.js v6 uses
private class fields (#field) which Proxies cannot access. This caused
"Cannot access private method" errors when calling provider methods
after MetaMask connection. Using shallowRef avoids deep proxying.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

commit.message:
Improve cover reliability across the app

- Replace all AsyncImage with SubcomposeAsyncImage to show a book icon
  placeholder when cover URLs fail to load (broken links, 404s, etc.)
- Add cover URL validation via HEAD request in BookRepository: reject
  non-image responses and tiny placeholder images (< 1KB) like Open
  Library's 1x1 pixel transparent GIF for missing covers
- Validate covers before saving so dead URLs never get stored
- Upscale Google Books thumbnails by setting zoom=0 (full resolution)
  and removing the edge-curl effect
- Apple Books fallback now only triggers after cover validation, so
  placeholder images don't block the fallback

Files changed: BookCard, BookGridCard, BookDetailScreen, ScannerScreen,
SpinnerScreen, AddBookScreen, BookApiModels, BookRepository

Co-Authored-By: Claude Sonnet 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

fix(voice-clone): raise socket.io maxHttpBufferSize to 50MB (#50)

Default maxHttpBufferSize is 1MB. Audio files for voice cloning
(base64-encoded) easily exceed this, causing socket.io to silently
drop the message — manifesting as the upload appearing to do nothing.

50MB accommodates the 30MB client-side file size cap plus base64 overhead.

Co-authored-by: Claude <noreply@anthropic.com>

feat(inbox): native dashboard inbox for human-agent interaction (#14)

* docs(proposals): add human-agent interaction proposal (issue + chat model)

Draft proposal to replace the one-way human_help_request handoff with a
threaded issue + chat interaction model, enabling bidirectional communication
and human-initiated task requests.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Sonnet 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

* docs(proposals): revise human-agent interaction — GitHub Issues as primary surface

- GitHub Issues is the interaction UI for all three shapes (A/B/C)
- Internal DB (threads + messages) is a sync mirror for fast local reads and persistence
- No dedicated chat page needed in initial scope
- Add _phase_sync_github_issues to agent cycle design
- Clarify polling latency is intentional (async system)
- Add GitHub token scope and issue label filtering to risks

Co-Authored-By: Claude Sonnet 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

* docs(plans): add implementation plan for human-agent interaction via GitHub Issues

Covers Shape A (agent opens issue when blocked) and Shape B (human opens
issue to initiate task). GitHub Issues is the primary UI; internal SQLite
threads+messages table mirrors content for fast local reads and dashboard
display. GitHub integration is entirely opt-in via environment variables.

Components:
- github/client.py — thin httpx wrapper over GitHub Issues REST API
- github/sync.py — per-cycle sync: mirror issues, return action lists
- storage/thread_store.py — threads + messages SQLite mirror
- core/models.py + store.py — Task.github_issue_url field + migration
- agent.py — _phase_sync_github_issues, open/close issues on state transitions
- dashboard/server.py — thread in task detail API response

Co-Authored-By: Claude Sonnet 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

* docs(plans): complete human-agent interaction plan with full design

- GitHub Issue state machine: 7 statuses (pending/linked/awaiting_human/
  human_responded/closed_completed/closed_cancelled/closed_abandoned)
- Full transition table covering all edge cases (human close, re-open,
  consecutive blocks, sub-task decomposition)
- Label conventions: sprout / needs-human / in-progress
- Issue/comment format templates for all states (blocked, still-blocked,
  completed, abandoned, cancelled)
- Auto-assign to GITHUB_ASSIGNEES on block (Shape A)
- 1:N data model: one issue → multiple tasks via thread_tasks junction table
- ThreadStore schema: threads, messages, thread_tasks, pending_comments
- GitHubClient API surface, sync.py SyncResult design
- Agent cycle integration: _phase_sync_github_issues before discovery
- Bootstrap opt-in via GITHUB_TOKEN/OWNER/REPO env vars

Co-Authored-By: Claude Sonnet 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

* docs(plans): add E2E test design using real Fullstop000/sprout repo

- Dedicated label sprout-e2e-test isolates test issues from production
- Tests skipped when GITHUB_TOKEN unset; tearDown closes all created issues
- Covers: create/list, comment round-trip, label management, close, since cursor
- Integration: Shape B pickup, Shape A unblock via human comment, outbox flush
- Unit tests (test_v2_*.py) and E2E tests (tests/e2e/) run independently

Co-Authored-By: Claude Sonnet 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

- Add ThreadStore (SQLite mirror of GitHub threads, messages, outbox)

commit.message:
추세 탭에 인터랙티브 SVG 꺾은선 차트 추가

주간/월간 토글 + 신규/성공/실패/잔여 체크박스로 동적 차트 표시.
건수/MRR 차트 각각 SVG로 렌더링, 상세 데이터 테이블 포함.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>
